GuidebookDescription: A service-sidearm built for close work. Easy to carry, quick to present, and dependable when a primary weapon is empty, jammed, or too loud to risk.
A compact automatic weapon meant for interior fights, breach defense, and panicked civilian militia use. It burns ammunition quickly but can stop rushes before they reach the line.
A general-purpose rifle carbine balanced for patrol, escort, and base defense. It is the first weapon that makes soldiers feel properly equipped.
A semi-automatic precision rifle meant to pick targets before they close. It rewards steady aim, clean sight lines, and trained military survivors.
A pump-action shotgun designed for brutal close engagements. It is loud enough to attract trouble, but it can end a breach at arm’s length.
A bolt-action precision rifle for long-range picks. It is slow, punishing, and expensive, but the first true answer to hard targets outside carbine range.
A long-range bolt-action sniper tuned for extreme distance. Heavy and slow to cycle, but optimized for high magnification optics and maximum penetration on hard targets.
